revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Solequelle]][[en:Brine spring]][[ja:塩化物泉]][[nn:Saltbrønn]]
{{Expand German|Solequelle|qq=}}
[[File:Sakinoyu Shirahama Hotspring.JPG|thumb|right|Example of a chloride spring, {{ill|Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|en|Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|qid=Q11408352|lt=Nanki Shirahama Onsen|1=Nanki-Shirahama Onsen}} "Saki no Yu"]] {{Redirect|Salt spring|qq=}} A '''brine spring''' or '''salt spring''' is a {{ill|Brine|en|Brine|qid=Q214403|lt=saltwater|1=Brine}} {{ill|Spring (hydrology)|en|Spring (hydrology)|qid=Q124714|lt=spring|1=Spring (hydrology)}}. Brine springs are not necessarily associated with {{ill|halite|en|halite|qid=Q5314|lt=halite|1=Halite}} deposits in the immediate vicinity. They may occur at valley bottoms made of clay and gravel which became soggy with brine seeped downslope from the valley sides.<ref>{{cite book|first=W.C.|last=Phalen|year=1919|title=Salt Resources of the United States|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=jagPAAAAIAAJ&pg=RA1-PA132|location=Washington, DC|publisher=Government Printing Office|page=132|oclc=4101987|qq=}} </ref> Historically, brine springs have been early sources of U.S. salt production, as in the case of the [[:en:salterns|salterns]] in {{ill|Syracuse, New York|en|Syracuse, New York|qid=Q128069|lt=Syracuse, New York|1=Syracuse, New York}} and at the {{ill|Illinois Salines|en|Illinois Salines|qid=Q16846607|lt=Illinois Salines|1=Illinois Salines}}. == In Japanese law == '''Chloride Spring''' (塩化物泉, enkabutsu-sen) is a classification of {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen Water|1=Onsen}} types based on the displayed spring quality name. It is categorized under {{ill|brine spring|lt=brine springs|ja|塩類泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/brine spring|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉}} among the {{ill|therapeutic springs|ja|療養泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic springs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉|1=Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q655311}}
== Overview == Brine springs typically contain {{ill|Sodium chloride|en|Sodium chloride|qid=Q2314|lt=sodium chloride|1=Sodium chloride}}(NaCl), commonly known as table salt, dissolved in the water. Due to its ability to prevent sweat evaporation, these springs help retain body heat, making bathers less likely to feel chilled. Brine springs also have antibacterial properties, which can aid in healing {{ill|Injury in humans|en|Injury in humans|qid=Q193078|lt=wounds|1=Injury in humans}} . Drinking the water from these springs often tastes salty due to the chloride content, and it may taste bitter if the concentration is high. Some chloride springs are classified as {{ill|Moor springs|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor hot springs|qq=}}<nowiki/>or may appear as {{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}} in certain cases. In springs with salt concentrations similar to {{ill|Seawater|en|Seawater|qid=Q184395|lt=seawater|1=Seawater}} , salt production businesses have historically operated. == Definition of Spring Quality == Brine springs are defined as having more than 1,000 mg of dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with [[chloride]] as the predominant anion. == Old and New Spring Quality Names == The table below compares old and new spring quality names: {| class="wikitable" |+ Correspondence Between Old and New Spring Quality Names<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=Reference Material 2: Comparison of Old and New Spring Quality Names |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Ministry of the Environment, Japan |accessdate=2018-03-26 }} </ref> ! Old Spring Quality Name !! New Spring Quality Name !! Abbreviated Spring Quality Name |- style="background-color:#eef" | Salt Spring || Sodium-Chloride Spring || Na-Cl Spring |- | Pure Salt Spring || Sodium-Chloride Spring || Na-Cl Spring |- | Carbonated Salt Spring || Carbonated-Sodium-Chloride Spring || Carbonated-Na-Cl Spring |- | Strong Salt Spring || Sodium-Chloride Strong Salt Spring || Na-Cl Strong Salt Spring |- | Weak Salt Spring || Sodium-Chloride Spring || Na-Cl Spring |- | Bicarbonate Salt Spring || Sodium-Chloride-Bicarbonate Spring || Na-Cl･HCO<sub>3</sub> Spring |- | Glauber Salt Spring || Sodium-Chloride-Sulfate Spring || Na-Cl･SO<sub>4</sub> Spring |- | Glauber and Bicarbonate Salt Spring || Sodium-Chloride-Sulfate-Bicarbonate Spring || Na-Cl･SO<sub>4</sub>･HCO<sub>3</sub> Spring |- | Earthy Salt Spring || Sodium-Magnesium (and Calcium)-Chloride Spring || Na･Mg(･Ca)-Cl Spring |- | Earthy Bicarbonate Salt Spring || Sodium-Calcium (and Magnesium)-Chloride-Bicarbonate Spring || Na･Ca(･Mg)-Cl･HCO<sub>3</sub> Spring |- | Gypsum Earthy Salt Spring || Sodium-Calcium-Chloride-Bicarbonate-Sulfate Spring || Na･Ca-Cl･HCO<sub>3</sub>･SO<sub>4</sub> Spring |- | Bromine Salt Spring || Sodium-Chloride Spring || Na-Cl Spring |- | Iodine Salt Spring || Iodine-Sodium-Chloride Spring || Iodine-Na-Cl Spring |- | Bromine and Iodine Salt Spring || Iodine-Sodium-Chloride Spring || Iodine-Na-Cl Spring |- | Boric Salt Spring || Sodium-Chloride Spring || Na-Cl Spring |} == Benefits == ''Note: Benefits are not guaranteed for everyone.'' The following are potential benefits based on the spring quality: === Indications === ==== For Bathing ==== In addition to {{ill|General Indications for hot spring use|ltt=general indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}} , these springs are effective for {{ill|cuts|ja|切り傷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cuts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 切創|1=cut|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4372969}} , {{ill|burns|ja|やけど|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熱傷|1=Burn|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q170518}} , chronic {{ill|skin diseases|ja|皮膚病|1=Dermatology|2=ja|3=皮膚病|4=de|5=Hautkrankheit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/skin diseases|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皮膚科学|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q171171}} , chronic {{ill|gynecological diseases|ja|婦人病|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gynecological diseases|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 婦人科学|1=Gynaecology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1221899}} , and {{ill|frail children|ja|虚弱児|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/frail children|1=frail children|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11625332}}
==== For Drinking ==== Indications include chronic {{ill|Constipation|en|Constipation|qid=Q178436|lt=constipation|1=Constipation}}<nowiki/>and chronic {{ill|digestive diseases|ja|消化器病|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/digestive diseases|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 消化器学|1=Gastroenterology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q120569}}
=== Contraindications === Bathing or drinking from chloride springs may be contraindicated in cases of {{ill|general contraindications|ja|一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/general contraindications|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一般的適応症}} . Drinking is specifically contraindicated for individuals with {{ill|Kidney disease|en|Kidney disease|qid=Q1054718|lt=kidney disease|1=Kidney disease}} , {{ill|hypertension|ja|高血圧症|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hypertension|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高血圧|1=Hypertension|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q41861}} , or {{ill|Edema|en|Edema|qid=Q152234|lt=edema|1=Edema}} . Additionally, drinking iodine-containing springs should be avoided in cases of {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|en|Hyperthyroidism|qid=Q16499|lt=hyperthyroidism|1=Hyperthyroidism}}
== Representative Hot Spring Locations == * {{ill|Asamushi Onsen|en|Asamushi Onsen|qid=Q4803496|lt=Asamushi Onsen|1=Asamushi Onsen}}({{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|qid=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Kaminoyama Onsen|ja|上山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminoyama Onsen|qq=}}({{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|qid=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Haneda Airport Garden|ja|羽田エアポートガーデン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Airport Garden|qq=}}({{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}) - Historically, the site of a major resort area. * {{ill|Atami Onsen|en|Atami Onsen|qid=Q11569090|lt=Atami Onsen|1=Atami Onsen}}({{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|ja|白浜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|1=Shirahama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11580028}}({{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Obama Onsen|en|Obama Onsen|qid=Q7074611|lt=Obama Onsen|1=Obama Onsen}}({{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|qid=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Senami Onsen|ja|瀬波温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Onsen|qq=}}({{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|qid=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Ibusuki Onsen|en|Ibusuki Onsen|qid=Q5984938|lt=Ibusuki Onsen|1=Ibusuki Onsen}}({{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|qid=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}) ==See also== *{{ill|Saline seep|en|Saline seep|qid=Q7404704|lt=Saline seep|1=Saline seep}} *[[:en:Salt lick|Salt lick]] *[[Mineral spring]] ==References== {{Reflist|qq=}} {{Authority control|qq=}} {{hydrology-stub|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} {{Onsen|qq=}}
